heading: Energy management requirements for United States Postal Service
label: 8262j Energy management requirements for United States Postal Service
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Energy Policy Act of 1992, and not as part of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Energy management requirements for postal facilities
children.label: a Energy management requirements for postal facilities

children.text: The Postmaster General shall, to the maximum extent practicable, ensure that each United States Postal Service facility meets the energy management requirements for Federal buildings and agencies specified in .section 8253 of this title

The Postmaster General may exclude from the requirements of such section any facility or collection of facilities, and the associated energy consumption and gross square footage if the Postmaster General finds that compliance with the requirements of such section would be impracticable. A finding of impracticability shall be based on the energy intensiveness of activities carried out in such facility or collection of facilities, the type and amount of energy consumed, or the technical feasibility of making the desired changes. The Postmaster General shall identify and list in the report required under the facilities designated by it for such exclusion.section 8262i of this title

children.label: A
children.text: describing how this section will be implemented;

children.label: B
children.text: designating personnel primarily responsible for achieving the requirements of this section; and
perform energy surveys of United States Postal Service facilities as necessary to achieve the requirements of this section;
install those energy conservation measures that will attain the requirements of this section in a cost-effective manner as defined in ; andsection 8254 of this title
ensure that the operation and maintenance procedures applied under this section are continued.
